# Prompt for Documentation Clean-Up (Copilot Optimization):

Make a deep dive analysis of the entire solution. 

Review all documentation files in the .github and docs folders. For each file:

1. dentify and remove outdated, redundant, or conflicting information.
2. Ensure all architectural, process, and code conventions are up-to-date and consistent with the current codebase.
3. Standardize terminology, formatting, and structure for clarity and searchability.
4. Add missing explanations for key patterns, modules, and decisions that Copilot or new contributors would need to understand the solution.
5. Remove or consolidate duplicate content across files.
6. Ensure all code snippets are correct, minimal, and reflect current best practices.
7. Clearly document the purpose and intended audience for each file.
8. Add or update a high-level index or README in each folder to guide navigation.

The goal is to make the documentation concise, accurate, and highly discoverable, so that Copilot can provide the most relevant and context-aware suggestions possible.

To make sure that all edge cases and nuances are well-documented, and that the documentation is structured in a way that allows for easy navigation and understanding by both humans and AI tools, consult 1 instances of gimini-3.1, 1 instances of gpt-5.4 and 1 instances of Claude Opus 4.6 to cross-verify the accuracy and completeness of the documentation. Use their feedback to further refine and optimize the content, ensuring that it is comprehensive and up-to-date with the latest developments in the codebase.

Involve all the agents and skills that is needed to accomplish this task, and make sure to use the most appropriate model for each step of the process. For example, use a model that is good at understanding and summarizing technical documentation for the review and clean-up process, and use a model that is good at generating clear and concise explanations for the missing explanations and high-level index.
